title: Learning Latent Dynamics for Planning from Pixels
key_contribution: Planning in learned latent dynamics from pixels
main_limitation: Limited visual realism and domain breadth by current standards
relevance_to_survey: Canonical bridge from video prediction to world models

title: Dream to Control: Learning Behaviors by Latent Imagination
key_contribution: Latent imagination with actor-critic learning
main_limitation: Still tied to task rewards and online interaction
relevance_to_survey: Essential for planning/control-oriented world-model taxonomy

title: Recurrent World Models Facilitate Policy Evolution
key_contribution: Popularized the modern world-model pipeline
relevance_to_survey: Foundational framing paper for the whole survey

title: Action-Conditional Video Prediction using Deep Networks in Atari Games
authors: Junhyuk Oh; Xiaoxiao Guo; Honglak Lee; Richard L. Lewis; Satinder Singh
key_contribution: Early long-horizon action-conditioned prediction from pixels
relevance_to_survey: Key origin point for predictive models from visual interaction

title: Unsupervised Learning for Physical Interaction through Video Prediction
authors: Chelsea Finn; Ian Goodfellow; Sergey Levine
key_contribution: Robot interaction learned through video prediction
relevance_to_survey: Core robot-world-model precursor

title: Stochastic Variational Video Prediction
key_contribution: Introduced latent-variable stochastic futures to video prediction
relevance_to_survey: Important for uncertainty in predictive world models

title: Interaction Networks for Learning about Objects, Relations and Physics
key_contribution: General-purpose learnable interaction simulator
main_limitation: Not video-based in its core formulation
relevance_to_survey: Foundational background for structured simulators and object interactions

title: Visual Interaction Networks
key_contribution: Combines visual perception with interaction-based physical dynamics
relevance_to_survey: One of the clearest visual-physics sequence models

title: Reasoning About Physical Interactions with Object-Oriented Prediction and Planning
key_contribution: Maps images to objects, predicts object interactions, renders back to pixels
main_limitation: Focused on structured synthetic object worlds
relevance_to_survey: Central object-centric world-model paper for intuitive physics

title: DINO-WM: World Models on Pre-trained Visual Features enable Zero-shot Planning
authors: Gaoyue Zhou; Hengkai Pan; Yann LeCun; Lerrel Pinto
key_contribution: Predicts DINOv2 features for zero-shot planning
relevance_to_survey: Important recent alternative to pixel reconstruction

title: Video Diffusion Models
key_contribution: Established diffusion as a serious video-model baseline
main_limitation: Not a world model in the control sense by itself
relevance_to_survey: Essential background for later diffusion world models

title: Diffusion for World Modeling: Visual Details Matter in Atari
key_contribution: Makes diffusion explicit as a world-model architecture
relevance_to_survey: Directly relevant modern diffusion-based world model

title: Genie: Generative Interactive Environments
key_contribution: Learns playable interactive environments from unlabeled video
main_limitation: Early foundation-world-model stage with many open questions
relevance_to_survey: High-value recent reference for interactive generative environments

title: Transformers are Sample-Efficient World Models
authors: Vincent Micheli; Eloi Alonso; François Fleuret
key_contribution: Recasts world modeling as token prediction with a transformer
main_limitation: Domain scope is still mainly Atari
relevance_to_survey: Important transformer-based world-model milestone

title: Mastering Atari with Discrete World Models
key_contribution: Shows strong Atari performance with discrete latent world models
relevance_to_survey: Key successor to Dreamer in discrete latent space

title: DayDreamer: World Models for Physical Robot Learning
key_contribution: Demonstrates world-model learning directly on physical robots
relevance_to_survey: Important embodied world-model paper

title: Contrastive Learning of Structured World Models
key_contribution: Uses contrastive learning to induce structured object-centric world models
relevance_to_survey: Very useful for structured latent-dynamics taxonomy

title: SlotFormer: Unsupervised Visual Dynamics Simulation with Object-Centric Models
key_contribution: Simulates dynamics in slot space for object-centric prediction
main_limitation: Still depends on object-centric pre-processing quality
relevance_to_survey: Strong modern object-centric dynamics reference

title: Entity Abstraction in Visual Model-Based Reinforcement Learning
authors: Rishi Veerapaneni; John D. Co-Reyes; Michael Chang; Michael Janner; Chelsea Finn; Jiajun Wu; Joshua B. Tenenbaum; Sergey Levine
key_contribution: OP3 is a key object-centric MBRL paper connecting unsupervised perception, dynamics, and planning
relevance_to_survey: Restored from ranked core paper list

title: MCVD: Masked Conditional Video Diffusion for Prediction, Generation, and Interpolation
authors: Vikram Voleti; Alexia Jolicoeur-Martineau; Christopher Pal
key_contribution: Direct diffusion formulation for video prediction and interpolation
main_limitation: Not yet a full planning/control world model
relevance_to_survey: Important diffusion-prediction bridge paper

title: VideoWorld: Exploring Knowledge Learning from Unlabeled Videos
key_contribution: Claims knowledge and control can emerge from unlabeled videos via latent dynamics
main_limitation: Very recent and broad in scope
relevance_to_survey: Potentially important for video-only world-model scaling

title: Vid2World: Crafting Video Diffusion Models to Interactive World Models
key_contribution: Transforms passive video diffusion models into interactive world models
main_limitation: Very recent and not yet broadly adopted
relevance_to_survey: Highly relevant newest paper at the diffusion/world-model boundary

title: PhyDNet: Disentangling Physical Dynamics From Unknown Factors for Unsupervised Video Prediction
key_contribution: Separates physical dynamics from residual appearance factors
main_limitation: Physics prior is still approximate and narrow
relevance_to_survey: Directly relevant to physical-dynamics learning from video

title: MaskViT: Masked Visual Pre-Training for Video Prediction
key_contribution: Masked-token video prediction scaled to planning use
main_limitation: Still primarily a predictive model rather than a full world model
relevance_to_survey: Useful bridge between video prediction and embodied planning

title: Eidetic 3D LSTM: A Model for Video Prediction and Beyond
key_contribution: Strong 3D recurrent architecture for spatiotemporal prediction
relevance_to_survey: Good representative of mature recurrent video prediction

title: SimVP: Simpler yet Better Video Prediction
key_contribution: Strong simple baseline for spatiotemporal prediction
relevance_to_survey: Useful baseline anchor in any survey section on later video prediction

title: Decomposing Motion and Content for Natural Video Sequence Prediction
key_contribution: Separates motion from content for prediction
relevance_to_survey: Important historical factorization idea

title: Stochastic Video Generation with a Learned Prior
key_contribution: Learns a prior over stochastic future trajectories
relevance_to_survey: Important stochastic latent-video reference

title: Learning Visual Predictive Models of Physics for Playing Billiards
key_contribution: Early object-based physical prediction from visuals
relevance_to_survey: Useful historical background for visual physics

title: A Compositional Object-Based Approach to Learning Physical Dynamics
key_contribution: Classic neural physics engine style compositional dynamics
relevance_to_survey: Important conceptual precursor to object-centric world models

title: Unsupervised Learning of Object Structure and Dynamics from Videos
key_contribution: Learns keypoint structure and dynamics without supervision
relevance_to_survey: Useful for object-centric representation learning from video

title: Pre-training Contextualized World Models with In-the-wild Videos for Reinforcement Learning
key_contribution: Pretrains world models on in-the-wild videos for transfer
relevance_to_survey: Important for large-scale video pretraining discussions

title: PHYRE: A New Benchmark for Physical Reasoning
key_contribution: Benchmark for physical reasoning and generalization
relevance_to_survey: Foundational benchmark background for physical world modeling

title: CLEVRER: CoLlision Events for Video REpresentation and Reasoning
key_contribution: Targets descriptive, explanatory, predictive, and counterfactual reasoning in videos
relevance_to_survey: Important for causal/physical reasoning evaluation

title: Physion: Evaluating Physical Prediction from Vision in Humans and Machines
key_contribution: Large-scale benchmark for visual physical prediction
relevance_to_survey: One of the most important physical-prediction benchmarks

title: Towards Accurate Generative Models of Video: A New Metric & Challenges
key_contribution: Introduced Fréchet Video Distance
main_limitation: Metric does not test controllability or physics directly
relevance_to_survey: Essential evaluation background for generative video sections

title: VideoPhy: Evaluating Physical Commonsense for Video Generation
key_contribution: Evaluates whether generated videos obey physical commonsense
main_limitation: Focused on commonsense prompts rather than full interaction
relevance_to_survey: Important modern physics benchmark for video generation

title: Towards World Simulator: Crafting Physical Commonsense-Based Benchmark for Video Generation
key_contribution: Builds a physics-commonsense benchmark and evaluation framework
main_limitation: Still benchmarking prompt-based generation rather than embodied control
relevance_to_survey: Useful for recent evaluation chapter and open-challenges discussion

title: How Far Is Video Generation from World Model: A Physical Law Perspective
key_contribution: Directly tests whether video models generalize physical laws
relevance_to_survey: Extremely relevant to the survey thesis itself

title: Do generative video models learn physical principles from watching videos?
key_contribution: Introduces Physics-IQ to disentangle realism from physical understanding
relevance_to_survey: High-value current reference on realism vs physics understanding
